channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Northern Territory Police release previously unseen investigation photos on 25th anniversary of Peter Falconio murder
item.description: Twenty-five years after the murder of British backpacker Peter Falconio in the remote Australian outback, Northern Territory Police have released a series of previously unseen photographs from the inv...

item.title: Green Party's Tayte Willows Wins P.E.I. By-Election
item.description: The Green Party's Tayte Willows has secured victory in the Prince Edward Island byelection, overcoming the incumbent Liberal leader. This result marks a significant shift in provincial politics, refle...

item.title: Stock Market Plummets as US-Iran Tensions Escalate, Oil Prices Rise
item.description: The Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) experienced a significant downturn as the KSE-100 index plummeted by over 6,000 points amid renewed tensions between the United States and Iran. The benchmark index o...

item.title: Overhauling the Pip System: A Progressive Approach to Disability Benefits
item.description: The article critiques the UK's disability benefits system, particularly the Personal Independence Payment (Pip), following the damning Timms report. It highlights how the current points-based assessme...

item.title: Madhya Pradesh UCC Panel Recommends Excluding Adivasis from Uniform Civil Code Bill
item.description: A committee formed to draft a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) for Madhya Pradesh, under BJP rule, has recommended excluding Adivasis from the proposed legislation. The UCC aims to establish a common legal fr...

item.title: Pharmacy Council Targets Unauthorized Medical Practices in Drug Shops
item.description: The Tanzania Pharmacy Council has launched a crackdown on pharmaceutical outlets operating beyond regulatory guidelines, aiming to protect public health. The initiative targets pharmacies and drug sho...

item.title: ICE's failure to secure crime scene after deadly shooting sparks Rachel Maddow's criticism
item.description: Rachel Maddow's Monday night show focused on the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) shooting of a Colombian man in Biddeford, Maine, which agents claimed was in self-defense but video evid...

item.title: Konami Adds Super Bomberman: Panic Bomber W to Collection with Free Update
item.description: Konami announced a free update for Super Bomberman Collection, set to release on August 20, which will add Super Bomberman: Panic Bomber W as a playable title. The collection currently features multip...

item.title: Australian government restricts Chinese investor influence in critical rare earth project
item.description: The Australian government has taken action to restrict the influence of Chinese-linked investors over a strategically important rare earth mining project in Western Australia. Treasurer Jim Chalmers h...

item.title: Chippa United Resolves Debt with Former Coach, Transfer Ban Lifted
item.description: Chippa United has resolved a R200,000 debt owed to former head coach Luc Eymael, which had resulted in a transfer ban from FIFA. The club settled the financial obligation, allowing them to resume inte...

item.title: President Marcos Inspects National Academy of Sports Facilities in New Clark City
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. recently inspected the facilities of the National Academy of Sports (NAS) at the New Clark City Sports Complex in Capas, Tarlac. Accompanied by Education Secretary Sonny...

item.title: South Africa's Business Struggles with Skills Gaps: Survey Highlights Key Shortages
item.description: A survey of 426 businesses in South Africa's Western Cape province reveals that skills shortages are a major barrier to growth. The study found that digital, soft, and environmental sustainability ski...

item.title: Former Corporator Remanded in Police Custody for Assault on Woman Doctor
item.description: A sessions court in Thane district has remanded former Shiv Sena Corporator Ramesh Mhatre in police custody for four days over allegations of assaulting a woman doctor at Shastrinagar hospital in Domb...

item.title: Market Volatility Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Earnings Reports
item.description: Global markets faced mixed performance as U.S.-Iran tensions escalated, spooking investors. Wall Street futures were mixed ahead of June inflation data, while TSX futures remained down following yeste...

item.title: NASA's Perseverance rover achieves marathon milestone on Mars
item.description: NASA's Perseverance rover has reached a significant milestone on Mars by completing the equivalent of a full marathon, driving 26.2 miles (42.195 kilometers) across the Red Planet. This accomplishment...

item.title: The Role of Local Ownership in Sustaining South Africa's Spaza Economy
item.description: The article highlights the critical role of spaza shops as an informal retail network that sustains millions of South Africans. With over 150,000 shops operating across townships and rural areas, this...

item.title: Prince Harry's Inheritance at Risk After Strained Relationship with King Charles
item.description: Prince Harry's recent visit to the UK has intensified tensions with his father, King Charles, potentially impacting his inheritance. The monarch is reportedly considering reducing Harry's share of his...

item.title: Four Terrorists Killed in Balochistan Operations, Total Eliminated Reaches 121 Since July 5
item.description: Security forces in Pakistan's Balochistan province have intensified anti-terror operations under 'Operation Shaban', eliminating 121 militants since July 5. The joint effort by the Army, Frontier Corp...

item.title: One Person Dies in Apartment Building Fire in Northeast Edmonton
item.description: A fire broke out in a mixed-use residential and commercial building in northeast Edmonton on Monday night, resulting in one fatality. Edmonton Fire Rescue Services received the call at 9:19 p.m. for a...

item.title: Nigeria denies US claim over seized electronic materials from terrorists
item.description: The Nigerian military has denied a US claim that a large amount of electronic materials was seized from terrorists in Nigeria. A US official stated that American forces recovered a significant cache o...
